security.description: API key management, auth polymorphism, input validation, credential safety
checks: SEC-01: API keys never hardcoded, always env vars
SEC-02: Auth headers polymorphic (x-api-key, Authorization, query param)
SEC-03: No credentials in JSON config file
SEC-04: Environment variables validated before use
SEC-05: Streaming responses don't leak sensitive data
SEC-06: Frontend doesn't store API keys in state/localStorage
SEC-07: CORS headers properly configured
SEC-08: Error messages don't expose backend details
SEC-09: Provider status health check is rate-limited
SEC-10: No SQL injection via provider names/model names

testing.description: Unit tests, integration tests, streaming correctness, error handling
checks: TEST-01: Backend routes have unit tests (llm_providers_routes.py)
TEST-02: Frontend component has integration tests (PromptForgeMultiLLM.jsx)
TEST-03: Streaming SSE parsing tested with mock responses
TEST-04: Provider fallback tested (1 provider fails, others continue)
TEST-05: Parallel comparison async/await correctness verified
TEST-06: Model loading dynamic fetch tested
TEST-07: Error boundaries prevent crashes
TEST-08: Token counter accuracy
TEST-09: Export JSON structure validated
TEST-10: i18n keys complete (FR/EN/BR) for all strings

architecture.description: Single source of truth, factory pattern extensibility, component design, config management
checks: ARCH-01: JSON config is single source of truth (no hardcoded provider data in code)
ARCH-02: llm_factory.get_llm() extensible for new providers
ARCH-03: API response schema consistent across all providers
ARCH-04: Streaming response format uniform (SSE with data: prefix)
ARCH-05: Frontend/backend contract documented (request/response examples)
ARCH-06: No circular dependencies between routes and factory
ARCH-07: Provider status enum standardized
ARCH-08: Async/await pattern consistent (no callback hell)
ARCH-09: Component state management (React hooks) properly organized
ARCH-10: Documentation (LLM_PROVIDERS_README.md) up-to-date

notes: Baseline cycle 1 — no prior scores to compare
Target global score: 90+ (excellent security + good testing + solid architecture)
If critical_checks fail, domain score capped at 50 regardless of other checks
Regression detection begins in cycle 2 (comparing against cycle 1 baseline)
